post.content:
## Introduction to Partially Funded Scholarships for Migrant Students
Partially funded scholarships for migrant students in the USA and Canada are designed to support undergraduate studies for international students with financial needs. These scholarships can help cover tuition fees, living expenses, and other education-related costs, making it easier for migrant students to pursue their academic goals. By applying for these scholarships, migrant students can reduce their financial burden and focus on their studies.

## Who Qualifies for Partially Funded Scholarships
To be eligible for partially funded scholarships, migrant students must hold a valid visa category, including:
* F-1 visa for academic students in the USA
* J-1 visa for exchange students in the USA
* F-2 visa for dependents of F-1 visa holders in the USA
* Study permit for international students in Canada
* Temporary Resident Visa (TRV) for international students in Canada

## Available Scholarships for Migrant Students
### University of British Columbia Scholarship
The University of British Columbia Scholarship provides $10,000 to $20,000 per year to international undergraduate students, including migrant students. To be eligible, students must have a minimum GPA of 3.0 and demonstrate financial need. The deadline for application is January 15, 2027. For more information, visit [https://you.ubc.ca](https://you.ubc.ca).

### Carleton University Scholarship
The Carleton University Scholarship offers $5,000 to $10,000 per year to international undergraduate students, including migrant students. To be eligible, students must have a minimum GPA of 2.5 and demonstrate financial need. The deadline for application is March 1, 2027. For more information, visit [https://carleton.ca](https://carleton.ca).

### University of Toronto Scholarship
The University of Toronto Scholarship provides $5,000 to $10,000 per year to international undergraduate students, including migrant students. To be eligible, students must have a minimum GPA of 3.0 and demonstrate financial need. The deadline for application is February 1, 2027. For more information, visit [https://www.utoronto.ca](https://www.utoronto.ca).

### New York University Scholarship
The New York University Scholarship offers $10,000 to $20,000 per year to international undergraduate students, including migrant students. To be eligible, students must have a minimum GPA of 3.0 and demonstrate financial need. The deadline for application is December 1, 2026. For more information, visit [https://www.nyu.edu](https://www.nyu.edu).

### University of California, Berkeley Scholarship
The University of California, Berkeley Scholarship provides $5,000 to $10,000 per year to international undergraduate students, including migrant students. To be eligible, students must have a minimum GPA of 2.5 and demonstrate financial need. The deadline for application is January 15, 2027. For more information, visit [https://www.berkeley.edu](https://www.berkeley.edu).

## Application Steps for Partially Funded Scholarships
To apply for partially funded scholarships, migrant students should follow these steps:
1. Research available scholarships and their eligibility criteria
2. Review the application requirements and deadlines
3. Prepare required documents, including transcripts and language proficiency tests
4. Submit the application form and supporting documents
5. Wait for the scholarship committee to review the application
6. Receive notification of the scholarship decision
7. Accept the scholarship offer and complete any additional requirements
8. Receive the scholarship funds and use them to support undergraduate studies

## Documents Checklist for Scholarship Applications
Migrant students should prepare the following documents to support their scholarship applications:
* Transcripts from previous academic institutions
* Language proficiency test results (e.g. TOEFL or IELTS)
* Proof of financial need (e.g. bank statements or tax returns)
* Letters of recommendation from academic or professional references
* Personal statement or essay outlining academic goals and motivations
* Passport and visa documents (e.g. F-1 visa or study permit)

## Frequently Asked Questions about Partially Funded Scholarships
Q: What is the difference between a fully funded and partially funded scholarship?
A: A fully funded scholarship covers all education-related costs, while a partially funded scholarship covers a portion of the costs. Partially funded scholarships can still provide significant financial support to migrant students.
Q: Can I apply for multiple scholarships at the same time?
A: Yes, migrant students can apply for multiple scholarships at the same time, but should review the eligibility criteria and application requirements carefully to ensure they meet the requirements for each scholarship.
Q: How do I demonstrate financial need for a scholarship application?
A: Migrant students can demonstrate financial need by providing proof of income, bank statements, or tax returns that show they have limited financial resources to support their undergraduate studies.
Q: What is the deadline for submitting a scholarship application?
A: The deadline for submitting a scholarship application varies depending on the scholarship and institution, but migrant students should check the official website or contact the scholarship committee for more information.
Q: Can I renew my scholarship for subsequent years of study?
A: Some scholarships can be renewed for subsequent years of study, but migrant students should review the scholarship terms and conditions to determine if renewal is possible.
Q: How do I accept a scholarship offer?
A: Migrant students can accept a scholarship offer by signing and returning the scholarship agreement, and completing any additional requirements outlined by the scholarship committee.
Q: What are the tax implications of receiving a scholarship?
A: The tax implications of receiving a scholarship vary depending on the country and institution, but migrant students should consult with a tax advisor or financial aid office to understand their tax obligations.

## External Resources for Migrant Students
For more information on studying in the USA, migrant students can visit the official website of the [US Department of Homeland Security](https://www.dhs.gov). For more information on studying in Canada, migrant students can visit the official website of [Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada](https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship.html).

## Conclusion
Partially funded scholarships can provide significant financial support to migrant students pursuing undergraduate studies in the USA and Canada. By researching available scholarships, following the application steps, and preparing required documents, migrant students can increase their chances of receiving a scholarship. For more information and resources on scholarships and studying abroad, visit [https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog](https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog).

related.content:
Are you a migrant student in the USA or Canada looking for partially funded awards to support your academic pursuits? You're in luck because there are numerous scholarships available to help you achieve your educational goals. In this post, we'll explore the world of partially funded awards for migrant students in the USA and Canada, focusing on academic achievement and providing you with the necessary information to get started.

## Introduction to Partially Funded Awards
Partially funded awards are a great way for migrant students to receive financial support for their education, helping to cover tuition fees, living expenses, and other related costs. These awards are typically offered by universities, organizations, and government agencies, and are designed to promote academic excellence and diversity. By applying for partially funded awards, migrant students can reduce their financial burden and focus on achieving their academic goals.

## Who Qualifies
To be eligible for partially funded awards, migrant students must meet certain criteria, including holding a valid visa. The following visa categories are typically eligible:
* F-1 visa (USA)
* J-1 visa (USA)
* F-2 visa (USA)
* J-2 visa (USA)
* Study permit (Canada)
* Temporary Resident Visa (Canada)
* Permanent Resident Visa (Canada)
* Refugee claimant documents (Canada)

## Available Scholarships
Here are five partially funded awards for migrant students in the USA and Canada:
### 1. Aga Khan Foundation International Scholarship
The Aga Khan Foundation International Scholarship provides funding of up to $50,000 per year to outstanding students from developing countries, including migrant students in the USA and Canada. To be eligible, applicants must be under the age of 30 and have a strong academic record. The deadline for application is March 31st, and the official website can be found at https://www.akdn.org/our-agencies/aga-khan-foundation/international-scholarship-programme.
### 2. Fulbright Foreign Student Program
The Fulbright Foreign Student Program offers funding of up to $30,000 per year to international students, including migrant students, to pursue a master's or doctoral degree in the USA. To be eligible, applicants must have a strong academic record and demonstrate leadership potential. The deadline for application is October 15th, and the official website can be found at https://foreign.fulbrightonline.org/.
### 3. Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarship
The Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarship provides funding of up to $50,000 per year to outstanding doctoral students, including migrant students, in Canada. To be eligible, applicants must have a strong academic record and demonstrate research potential. The deadline for application is November 1st, and the official website can be found at https://vanier.gc.ca/en/home-accueil.html.
### 4. Paul and Daisy Soros Fellowships for New Americans
The Paul and Daisy Soros Fellowships for New Americans offer funding of up to $90,000 over two years to outstanding migrant students in the USA. To be eligible, applicants must be under the age of 31 and have a strong academic record. The deadline for application is November 1st, and the official website can be found at https://www.pdsoros.org/.
### 5. Ontario Graduate Scholarship
The Ontario Graduate Scholarship provides funding of up to $15,000 per year to outstanding graduate students, including migrant students, in Ontario, Canada. To be eligible, applicants must have a strong academic record and demonstrate research potential. The deadline for application is February 1st, and the official website can be found at https://osap.gov.on.ca/eng/notol_003157.html.

## Application Steps
To apply for partially funded awards, follow these steps:
1. Research available scholarships and their eligibility criteria.
2. Review the application requirements and deadlines.
3. Prepare your application materials, including transcripts, letters of recommendation, and a personal statement.
4. Submit your application through the official website or mail.
5. Wait for the selection committee to review your application.
6. Receive notification of the outcome, either by email or mail.
7. Accept the award and follow any instructions provided.
8. Use the funding to support your academic pursuits.

## Documents Checklist
When applying for partially funded awards, make sure to have the following documents ready:
* Valid visa or study permit
* Passport
* Transcripts
* Letters of recommendation
* Personal statement
* Proof of language proficiency (if required)
* Proof of financial need (if required)

## Frequently Asked Questions
Here are some common questions migrant students ask when applying for partially funded awards:
1. What is the difference between a partially funded award and a fully funded scholarship?
2. Can I apply for multiple scholarships at the same time?
3. How do I demonstrate financial need for a partially funded award?
4. Can I use a partially funded award to study online or part-time?
5. What is the typical deadline for applying to partially funded awards?
6. How do I apply for a partially funded award if I don't have a valid visa?
7. Can I renew a partially funded award for multiple years?
8. What are the tax implications of receiving a partially funded award?

For more information on partially funded awards, visit the official government website of the USA at https://www.ed.gov/ or the official website of the University of Toronto at https://www.utoronto.ca/.

In conclusion, partially funded awards are a great way for migrant students to receive financial support for their education in the USA and Canada, and by following the application steps and preparing the necessary documents, you can increase your chances of success - check our blog for more information https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog
